reforms.description: The Dutch government has established a broad-based innovation pillar that includes entrepreneurship, research, and knowledge clustering, with a total funding of €2.9 billion during its term. This initiative aims to enhance innovative activity and improve educational outcomes across the education system.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The funding is expected to stimulate innovation and entrepreneurship, leading to improved productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has allocated significant funding to support innovation as a key pillar of its economic strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in the approach to innovation policy, with substantial funding aimed at enhancing the innovation ecosystem in the Netherlands.
reforms.source_quote: The government has created a broad-based innovation pillar by including, among other elements, entrepreneurship, research, and clustering of knowledge and education.

reforms.description: To foster entrepreneurship, the government has allocated an additional €450 million to expand the innovation voucher system for SMEs, allowing them to purchase R&D services from knowledge centres.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating access to R&D services, the voucher system is expected to enhance innovation capabilities among SMEs, contributing to overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has allocated additional funding for expanding the innovation voucher system.
reforms.importance_rationale: This expansion of the voucher system is a significant enhancement of an existing instrument aimed at supporting SME innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government has allocated an additional €450 million for spending on, among other things, expanding the innovation voucher system for SME to purchase R&D from knowledge centres.

reforms.description: The government has allocated €250 million to strengthen regional cluster programmes, recognizing the positive effects of clustering on innovative activity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Strengthening regional clusters is expected to enhance collaboration and innovation among firms, leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has allocated funding to strengthen regional cluster programmes.
reforms.importance_rationale: This funding represents a significant investment in enhancing regional innovation ecosystems, which can have lasting impacts on local economies.
reforms.source_quote: The government has allocated €250 million to strengthen regional cluster programmes.

reforms.description: The Dutch government aims to stimulate research, innovation, and entrepreneurship by increasing the number of innovation vouchers for small and medium-sized companies to purchase research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing financial support for research purchases, the initiative encourages innovation and enhances the competitiveness of small and medium-sized enterprises.
reforms.status_evidence: The government aims at stimulating research, innovation and entrepreneurship.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant effort to enhance innovation support for SMEs, which is crucial for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The direct innovation measures include increasing the number of innovation vouchers (for small and medium-sized companies to purchase research).

reforms.description: The government plans to extend wage subsidies for research and development activities for small companies to further encourage innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing the cost of R&D for small firms, this measure is expected to incentivize more investment in innovation, thereby enhancing productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The direct innovation measures include extending wage subsidies for research and development activities for small companies.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it directly supports small companies in their R&D efforts, which is vital for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The direct innovation measures include extending wage subsidies for research and development activities for small companies.

reforms.description: A micro-credit arrangement for start-up entrepreneurs is to be introduced to facilitate access to funding for new businesses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative aims to lower the barriers to entry for new businesses, promoting entrepreneurship and innovation in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: Entrepreneurship is to be stimulated by introducing a micro-credit arrangement for start-up entrepreneurs.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is important as it directly addresses the funding challenges faced by start-ups, which are crucial for innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: Entrepreneurship is to be stimulated by introducing a micro-credit arrangement for start-up entrepreneurs.

reforms.description: The Dutch government has targeted an additional investment of €300 million from the gas revenue fund to strengthen research infrastructure through public-private partnerships, enhancing linkages between firms and knowledge institutes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Strengthening research infrastructure fosters innovation and enhances the capacity of firms to develop new products and services.
reforms.status_evidence: The investment has been made to strengthen research infrastructure.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant investment in research infrastructure, which is crucial for enhancing innovation capacity and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: An additional investment of €300 million from the gas revenue fund (FES) to strengthen the research infrastructure through public-private partnerships.

reforms.description: The government has made university funding partly dependent on performance in the diffusion of knowledge to firms, redistributing €50 million of basic funding based on scientific performance and valorisation of knowledge gains.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Linking funding to knowledge diffusion encourages universities to enhance their collaboration with industry, thereby promoting innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: Funding has been redistributed based on performance metrics.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform fundamentally changes the funding model for universities, incentivizing them to engage more with industry and enhance their role in innovation.
reforms.source_quote: In 2006 €50 million of the basic funding of universities was redistributed dependent on scientific performance and valorisation of knowledge gains.

reforms.description: The government has intensified education programmes in favour of entrepreneurship since November 2005, aiming to enhance the diffusion of innovation and support the development of entrepreneurial skills among students.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering entrepreneurial skills, the initiative is expected to stimulate innovation and economic growth through new business creation.
reforms.status_evidence: Education programmes in favour of entrepreneurship have been intensified from November 2005 onwards.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to enhance the skills of future entrepreneurs, which is crucial for fostering innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: Education programmes in favour of entrepreneurship have been intensified from November 2005 onwards.

reforms.description: The Dutch government enacted a reform of the childcare subsidy system in 2005, allowing for a more flexible supply of childcare services by providing income-dependent benefits to households purchasing these services, rather than subsidizing providers directly.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing access to affordable childcare, the reform encourages higher female labor force participation, which can enhance overall productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform was enacted to improve the flexibility and affordability of childcare services.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in childcare policy, aiming to enhance labor market participation and address barriers faced by working parents, particularly women.
reforms.source_quote: A reform of the childcare subsidy system was enacted in 2005 by the central government to allow a more flexible supply of childcare services.

reforms.description: In 2004, the Netherlands introduced a scheme to attract highly-skilled workers, exempting those earning over €45,000 from the work permit requirement, aimed at stimulating innovation in the economy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This scheme aims to enhance the supply of skilled labor in knowledge-intensive sectors, thereby fostering innovation and productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme has been increasingly used, with nearly 4000 entry visas granted to knowledge workers in 2006.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of this scheme represents a significant policy change aimed at attracting skilled labor, which is crucial for enhancing the innovation capacity of the Dutch economy.
reforms.source_quote: In 2004, a scheme to attract highly-skilled workers was introduced following the advice of the Innovation Platform, established by the government to design policies aimed at stimulating innovation in the Dutch economy.
